pain_point_label: Inadequate Access to Assistive Technology & Materials

matches.match_rationale: Text-to-speech (TTS) directly replaces costly audiobook licenses and screen-reader software for visually impaired and dyslexic students. A multi-site operator can deploy a single TTS pipeline that converts any curriculum text into accessible audio on demand, eliminating per-title audiobook procurement costs. This is technically mature, low-cost via API, and requires no custom model training.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A platform that ingests any curriculum document (PDF, DOCX) and generates natural-sounding audio narrations for dyslexic and visually impaired students, replacing expensive commercial audiobook and screen-reader licenses across all campuses.

matches.match_rationale: LLMs can auto-generate adapted versions of curriculum materials — simplified language for cognitive disabilities, large-print formatting instructions, and plain-text equivalents for tactile learning guides — replacing the manual labor of specialist staff adapting each document. Multi-site operators pay staff per hour to manually adapt materials; AI dramatically reduces that unit cost. This directly addresses the budget constraint on adapted materials.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI adaptation engine that takes standard curriculum text and produces multiple accessible variants (simplified language, large-print-ready, symbol-augmented) at near-zero marginal cost, scaling across all campuses without additional specialist headcount.

matches.match_rationale: OCR and document parsing can convert printed or scanned curriculum materials into machine-readable text, which is the prerequisite step before TTS, large-print reformatting, or Braille translation. Many education operators have legacy printed resources that cannot be adapted until digitized. This removes a key bottleneck for hearing-impaired and visually impaired students who need digital-first formats.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A document ingestion pipeline that scans and OCRs all printed curriculum materials into structured digital text, feeding downstream accessible format generators and eliminating the manual re-typing burden on support staff.

matches.match_rationale: An end-to-end accessible materials pipeline — ingest curriculum document, detect student disability profile, select adaptation type (audio, simplified text, large print), generate the adapted artifact, and deliver to the student — requires multi-step orchestration with branching logic that goes beyond single-step generation. This automation reduces the specialist staff time spent managing each accommodation request across multiple campuses. It also creates an auditable workflow that satisfies compliance requirements around SEN accommodations.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous accommodation fulfilment agent that accepts a teacher or coordinator's request, pulls the relevant curriculum content, applies the correct accessibility transformation for the student's profile, and delivers the adapted material — replacing a multi-day manual process with a minutes-long automated one.
